slides.title: Wonderful Water
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 1 English, Unit 8: Wonderful Water. In this unit, your child learns about water — where it comes from, where we find it, and all the ways we use it every day. They meet rainy and sunny weather, animals that live in water, and the big idea that water is a gift we should not waste. The goal is warm, confident English and a thankful heart for water — not perfect spelling. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Say why plants, animals, and people all need water
Name places where we find water (rain, river, sea, well)
Say how we use water: “I use water to drink / wash / cook.”
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to say why plants, animals, and people all need water. you will also name places where we find water (rain, river, sea, well). you will also say how we use water: “i use water to drink / wash / cook.”. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: water, rain, drop, wet, dry, clean. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes The Well in the Village and Talk about Wonderful Water. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Language pattern 1
bullets: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I use water to ___.
slides.narration: The first language focus is Language pattern 1. Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I use water to ___. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 - Listen and point. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
